\section{Case studies}
We use Hector to build three synthesis approaches: an HLS tool, a systolic array generator, and an accelerator component for sparse linear algebra. These case studies are chosen to show how Hector can be used to build various synthesis tools. %Furthermore, we want to demonstrate that Hector makes it possible to flexibly combine different approaches. The details of these case studies are explained below.

\input{hls}
\input{Systolic Array}
\input{Sparse}
% \subsection{Discussion}
% These case studies show a variety of synthesis techniques, including different HLS scheduling strategies, domain-specific approaches, and a hybrid HLS/RTL design. All of these methods can be well supported in Hector with minimal engineering effort. Hector also allows for the flexible combination of multiple methodologies, which is difficult to achieve at the RTL level.

\input{Relatedwork}
